markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define lease expiry, orphan reclaim, cancellation, and retry semantics for long-running model/index tasks. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: AgentLeaseExpiration leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: AgentLeaseExpiration is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 e2212884; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: STRONG CORE: server-side expiry is DB-clock enforced, NOT app-clock spoofable. is_expired is computed as '(expires_at_utc < NOW())' in SQL (storage/knowledge_crdt.rs:258 LEASE_COLUMNS), evaluated at every read. No client timestamp ever feeds expiry. renew_lease refuses an already-expired lease via 'AND expires_at_utc > NOW()' (knowledge_crdt.rs:392). takeover_lease runs FOR UPDATE inside a tx and re-checks is_expired (knowledge_crdt.rs:457-535), so no double-grant: the partial unique index uq_knowledge_crdt_lease_active_scope (0151:46-48, WHERE released_at_utc IS NULL) makes a second active holder a unique violation -> ScopeHeld.

ON-DEMAND SWEEP WINDOW CLOSED: the worry that 'an expired lease still authorizes a write before the sweep stamps it' does NOT hold here, because guard_lease_for_write does not depend on expired_at_utc being stamped. It reads is_expired (live DB-clock comparison) via get_lease and denies LeaseExpired (agent_lease.rs:416-419). The sweep (sweep_expired_leases) only emits LEASE_EXPIRED events + stamps expired_at_utc for observability; it is NOT on the authorization path. So expiry is enforced even with the sweep never run.

ARCHITECTURAL GAP (medium): guard_lease_for_write is an ADVISORY function, not an enforced chokepoint. Nothing structurally forces an authority/draft write to pass through it. save_rich_document_draft (save_semantics.rs / push_yjs_update) does NOT call guard_lease_for_write at all -- draft saves are ungated by leases. record_ai_edit_proposal (MT-074) only checks lease_id.is_some() (FK guarantees existence) but never calls the guard, so an expired/foreign/wrong-scope but EXISTING lease id satisfies the 'must hold a lease' rule. The migration header asserts 'a lease MUST be claimed before a model session mutates ... drafts ... proposals' but the code only enforces lease presence (FK), not lease validity, on the proposal paths. The guard is only meaningful if a caller chooses to invoke it.
Takeover lineage walk lease_lineage caps at 256 links and errors on overflow (knowledge_crdt.rs:548-552) -- good loop-cap hygiene.
evasion_probes: Tried to forge expiry via client clock: impossible, expires_at_utc is set server-side NOW()+make_interval (knowledge_crdt.rs:306) and compared server-side; client never supplies a timestamp.
Tried renew of expired lease: blocked by 'expires_at_utc > NOW()' predicate; returns None (knowledge_crdt.rs:392).

Tried write under foreign lease: guard denies ForeignLease when lease.actor_id != writer.canonical() (agent_lease.rs:420) -- BUT only if guard is actually called; the proposal insert paths do not call it.

Tried double-grant via concurrent takeover of same expired lease: FOR UPDATE row lock + partial unique index serialize it; second loses on unique violation or sees released prior. No orphan/double-grant found.
Searched all call sites of guard_lease_for_write outside tests: none wired into save/proposal/promotion production flows; the guard exists but is not mandatory.
evidence_checked: Cross-checked save_semantics.rs and ai_edit_proposal.rs for guard invocation: not invoked

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
